codeReview.summary: The reviewed handoff claims secure-field metadata, metadata secrecy boundaries, slimmer nested trait bounds, and approved-wrapper compile boundaries were already implemented on commit 05ee508, but that commit only adds recursive nested secure shapes plus runtime/store roundtrip coverage. It does not add SECURE_FIELDS metadata exposure, metadata-boundary assertions, or the nested trait-bound proof required by the feature contract.

issues.description: The only secure-field metadata test lives after commit 05ee508, so the reviewed commit itself does not prove or implement VAL-NS-006/VAL-NS-007. The feature handoff incorrectly attributes SECURE_FIELDS metadata exposure and secrecy-boundary coverage to commit 05ee508.

The nested trait-bound compile-pass proof required for reduced Encrypted* trait burden (VAL-NS-008) is not part of commit 05ee508; it was added later in commit f596cf4 via tests/ui/sensitive/nested_trait_bounds_compile_pass.rs. The reviewed feature therefore did not satisfy its stated trait-bound contract at that commit.

The commit preserves the unsupported-wrapper compile-fail boundary only through a generic HashMap fixture and message text. It does not add focused compile-fail coverage for wrapper-form boundaries beyond the approved Child/Option<Child>/Vec<Child> set, so the handoff overstates how specifically the wrapper boundary was locked at this stage.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rust-library-worker session finished in verification-only mode and reported success for this feature even though the commit under review did not contain all promised assertions. The skill guidance allows verification-only completion for already-landed work, but here that shortcut masked a contract mismatch between the assigned feature and the actual commit contents.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff 2026-03-24T18-35-21-349Z__nested-sensitive-metadata-and-bounds__8f8c286b-2f2f-494b-98bc-980326db7578.json says no code changes were needed and cites commit 05ee508; git log shows later commits f596cf4 and 565419e were needed to add the missing nested trait-bound and override proofs.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Mission shared state does not clearly record which validation assertions were only satisfied by later fix commits versus the original feature commit, which made it easy for the worker to mis-attribute later metadata/bounds behavior to 05ee508.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json assigns VAL-NS-006/007/009 to nested-sensitive-metadata-and-bounds, but VAL-NS-008 was later split into nested-sensitive-metadata-proof-fix after scrutiny. The original handoff still describes the broader contract as if 05ee508 already covered it.

summary: Review failed: commit 05ee508 implemented nested recursive secure shapes, but it did not yet contain the secure-field metadata surface and focused nested trait-bound proof that the feature handoff claims. The report for this feature overstates the implementation status at that commit.
